zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Spencer Beaucamp於1526年出生在英國武斯特斯爾德雷頓時，她的父親William Beauchamp41歲，母親Amy Haworth41歲。她於1545年8月9日與John Wakeman在英國武斯特斯爾貝德利結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1587年3月27日在英國武斯特斯爾貝德利享年61歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可霍林格 姆 易可沃斯聖 萊昂納達斯 亞德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Spencer Beaucamp于1526年出生在英国武斯特斯爾德雷頓时，她的父亲William Beauchamp41岁，母亲Amy Haworth41岁。她于1545年8月9日与John Wakeman在英国武斯特斯爾貝德利结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1587年3月27日在英国武斯特斯爾貝德利享年61岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可霍林格 姆 易可沃斯聖 萊昂納達斯 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Beaucamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: Spencer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin an occupational name for a ‘dispenser’ of supplies in a manor house. This is the name of a great English noble family, traditionally supposed to be descended from someone who performed this function in the royal household. Its popularity as a given name was influenced in the mid-20th century by the American film actor  Spencer Tracy  (  1900–67 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Spencer Beaucamp was born in 1526, in Drayton, Worcestershire, England, her father, William Beauchamp, was 41 and her mother, Amy Haworth, was 41. She married John Wakeman on 9 August 1545, in Bewdley, Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 27 March 1587, in Bewdley, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in Saint Leonard's Churchyard, Horringer cum Ickworth, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé John Wakeman le 9 août 1545, à Bewdley, Worcestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 27 mars 1587, à Bewdley, Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterrée à Saint Leonard's Churchyard, Horringer cum Ickworth, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Spencer Beaucampが1526年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアドレイトンで生まれた時，父William Beauchampは41歳，母Amy Haworthは41歳であった。1545年8月9日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアベウドリーでJohn Wakemanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1587年3月27日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアベウドリーで61歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクホーリンジャー クウム イックウオルスサイント レオナルド'ス チャーチヤルドにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint Leonard's Churchyard, Ribbesford,: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 호링거 c 이ck워스 세인트 리나르드'스 c후르c히아어드
ko.lifeSummary: Joan Spencer Beaucamp(이)가 1526년 영국 워스스터실르 드라이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Beauchamp(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Amy Haworth(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 1545년 8월 9일 영국 워스스터실르 브w들리에서 John Wakeman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1587년 3월 27일 영국 워스스터실르 브w들리에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 호링거 c 이ck워스 세인트 리나르드'스 c후르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

pt.Saint Leonard's Churchyard, Ribbesford,: Saint Leonard's Churchyard, Horringer cum Ickworth, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Spencer Beaucamp nasceu em 1526, em Drayton, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Beauchamp, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Amy Haworth, tinha 41 anos. Ela casou-se com John Wakeman em 9 de agosto de 1545, em Bewdley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 27 de março de 1587, em Bewdley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, com 61 anos, e foi sepultada em Saint Leonard's Churchyard, Horringer cum Ickworth, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Saint Leonard's Churchyard, Ribbesford,: сеинт леонард'с чурчярд, хауррингир кум иккворт, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Spencer Beaucamp родилась в 1526 г. в местности дреитон, ворсестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Beauchamp было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Amy Haworth, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Wakeman 9 августа 1545 г. в местности бюдли, ворсестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 27 марта 1587 г. в местности бюдли, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 61 год и была похоронена в местности сеинт леонард'с чурчярд, хауррингир кум иккворт, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Saint Leonard's Churchyard, Ribbesford,: Saint Leonard's Churchyard, Horringer cum Ickworth, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Spencer Beaucamp nació en 1526, en Drayton, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Beauchamp, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Amy Haworth, tenía 41 años. Se casó con John Wakeman el 9 de agosto de 1545, en Bewdley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 27 de marzo de 1587, en Bewdley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultada en Saint Leonard's Churchyard, Horringer cum Ickworth, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
